task_statement: CR.v4 P3 hard fan-in template locality repair. Current 0629 evidence shows the old runtime blocker is repaired: fan-in source QA transition_concern_evidence stays advisory/local and raw Link movement remains forward through closure. Remaining Brick-axis delta: QA Brick prose and checker/profile wording must align with return.yaml and runtime meaning. Do not change engine semantics unless a focused checker proves a contradiction. Keep full Brick template required_return_shape; do not remove transition_concern_evidence from code-attack-qa or axis-attack-qa return.yaml. Clarify that QA/Inspector source-lane transition_concern_evidence is non-binding local Agent evidence and is not Link-facing before closure-synthesis; carries_forward_fields stays the carry filter; closure-synthesis remains the Link-facing concern source. Add or adjust focused checker/profile coverage only if needed to prove manual graph_packet and brick-protocol-engine-feature-hard materialization preserve this meaning. Preserve official route: no --large, no helper route, no manual required_return_shape/carry injection. QA may inspect/probe only and must not source-mutate. Closure reports FORWARD/HOLD/REROUTE/NOT_PROVEN as support evidence only.

nodes.work_statement: Design the narrow repair from the 3-axis evidence. Brick: QA Brick prose/return.yaml/checker wording; Agent: QA returned concern facts are local/non-binding; Link: fan-in source concerns are not adopted before closure-synthesis. Produce exact file plan. Do not edit code in this design step.

Implement the narrow P3 hard fan-in template locality repair. Primary targets: brick/templates/bricks/code-attack-qa/brick.md and brick/templates/bricks/axis-attack-qa/brick.md. Align prose with return.yaml: transition_concern_evidence remains in required_return_shape but is local/non-binding Agent evidence for fan-in source QA lanes; it is not Link-facing before closure-synthesis; carries_forward_fields filters it out; closure/closure-synthesis may be Link-facing under declared policy. If checker/profile wording is contradictory, update the minimal checker/profile text or assertion under support/checkers/lib/case_runners.py and support/checkers/profiles/building_skill_preset_agent_tool_hardening.yaml. Do not remove return.yaml fields, do not change Link Movement vocabulary, do not add --large, do not create new route/runtime/gate/scheduler. Run focused checks where possible.

Codex code/regression QA. Inspect the diff and focused checker evidence. Verify return.yaml fields were not removed; QA Brick prose says source-lane transition_concern_evidence is local/non-binding before closure; checker/profile wording matches full template return shape plus carry filtering/advisory adoption; run git diff --check and focused profile/checker where possible. Do not source-mutate.

Gemini-local axis/evidence QA. Attack Brick/Agent/Link boundaries. Check that Brick templates own return shape, Agent returns local/non-binding facts, and Link alone owns Movement/adoption. Verify support/checker wording does not make support or QA a Movement authority. Do not source-mutate; return exact not_proven if provider inspection is limited.

Codex closure. Fan in design, work, Codex QA, and Gemini axis QA. Report FORWARD/HOLD/REROUTE/NOT_PROVEN as support evidence only. HOLD if QA source-mutated, if return.yaml fields were removed, if checker/profile evidence is missing, or if the wording still implies QA source-lane concerns are Link-facing before closure-synthesis. Do not claim success, quality, source truth, or Movement authority.
